• Cost of additional infra (CPU, DB I/O). | Expected incremental volume ≥ ₹8 bn (≈ 66 % of forecast) with ≤ 5 % cost uplift → Go. | | **System health** | • API error rate (5xx).
• CPU/Memory utilisation spikes. | Sustained error < 0.1 % and resource utilisation < 70 % of capacity → Go; otherwise scale‑up required. | | **Qualitative** | • NPS/CSAT on repeat flow (post‑transaction survey).
• Qualitative feedback from pilot support tickets. | NPS ≥ +30 and > 80 % “ease‑of‑use” rating reinforce product‑market fit. | **Decision matrix** (simplified): | Condition | Outcome | |-----------|---------| | All KPI thresholds met (adoption, success, risk, revenue) | **Full‑scale launch** (feature flag rolled out to 100 % of users). | | One KPI fails but root cause is remedial (e.g., latency high due to temporary load) | **Iterate MVP**, re‑run pilot after fix. | | Any risk/compliance KPI breaches (fraud loss > ₹0.5 M or grievance > 0.1 %) | **No‑Go** – redesign risk controls before any broader rollout. | The MVP therefore serves as a data‑driven validation gate, delivering the exact quantitative evidence NPCI needs to certify that the One‑Tap Repeat feature can be scaled safely, compliantly, and profitably across the entire UPI ecosystem.

sections.title: 6. Product Operating
sections.content:
## 3 Success KPIs | # | KPI | Target | Measurement Horizon | |---|-----|--------|----------------------| | 1 | **One‑Tap Repeat Adoption** – % of active UPI users who execute ≥1 repeat‑pay transaction per month | **≥ 25 %** of the monthly active user base | By **Month 6** post‑launch (Q2 FY‑26) | | 2 | **Transaction Success Rate** – Successful repeat‑pay completions / total repeat‑pay attempts | **≥ 99.5 %** (same as overall UPI success benchmark) | Measured **monthly** from Day 0 | | 3 | **Trust / Grievance Rate** – Number of repeat‑pay related complaints / total repeat‑pay transactions | **< 0.1 %** (≤ 1 complaint per 1,000 repeat payments) | Evaluated **monthly**; target to be met by **Month 3** and sustained thereafter | --- ## Grievance redressal (Trust) | Aspect | Detail | |--------|--------| | **Channel** | Integrated with NPCI’s existing **Grievance Management Portal (GMP)** and the UPI‑App‑Level “Help → Dispute” flow. | | **Automation** | – Auto‑generation of a ticket ID when a user selects “Dispute this repeat payment”.
– Real‑time linkage to the original transaction ID, payee details, and risk‑score snapshot. | | **SLA – Acknowledgement** | 24 hours from ticket creation (system‑generated acknowledgment SMS/WhatsApp). | | **SLA – Resolution** | 48 hours for “simple‑dispute” cases (e.g., duplicate repeat, wrong amount).
Escalation to **RBI‑mandated dispute board** within 5 working days for “fraud‑suspected” cases. | | **Escalation Matrix** | Tier‑1 (GMP Ops) → Tier‑2 (NPCI Risk & Compliance) → Tier‑3 (RBI/Bank Ombudsman). | | **Metrics** | – First‑Contact Resolution (FCR) ≥ 85 %
– Average Resolution Time ≤ 48 h
– Grievance‑Rate KPI (see above). | | **Communication** | Proactive status updates via push‑notification and email; final outcome shared with a “refund/charge‑back” option as per RBI UPI guidelines. | --- ## Day 0 automation | Process | Automated from Day 0 | Manual / Human‑in‑the‑Loop (initial) | |---------|----------------------|--------------------------------------| | **Capture of last successful transaction** | Auto‑stored in the “Repeat‑Pay Cache” (encrypted, PCI‑DSS compliant). | – | | **Generation of a one‑tap repeat token** | System creates a **single‑use, time‑bound token** linked to the original VPA and amount. | – | | **UI rendering of One‑Tap widget** | Dynamic placement on home‑screen & post‑payment receipt via UI‑Engine. | – | | **Risk‑score evaluation** | Real‑time call to existing **Fraud‑Score Engine**; score attached to token. | – | | **Settlement posting** | Same settlement pipeline as regular UPI; auto‑posted to the **Settlement & Guarantee Fund (SGF)**. | – | | **Monitoring & alerting** | Auto‑alert on token misuse, abnormal repeat‑pay frequency (> 5 repeats/day). | Initial **manual review** of alerts for the first 2 weeks; thereafter fully automated with periodic audit. | | **Exception handling (e.g., insufficient balance)** | Auto‑reject with standard UPI error code; push notification to user. | – | | **Customer support ticket creation** | Auto‑opened when a repeat‑pay fails with “User‑initiated dispute” flag. | – | --- ## Impact on SGF | Impact Area | Explanation | |------------|-------------| | **Settlement flow** | No change – repeat‑pay uses the same **ReqPay/RespPay** settlement path; funds settle in the same T+0 window. | | **SGF contribution** | SGF contribution is a % of net settlement value (currently 2 %). With an incremental **₹12 bn** annual repeat‑pay volume, SGF exposure rises by **≈ ₹240 cr** annually – well within the existing SGF capacity (≈ ₹1.8 tr). | | **Liquidity risk** | The one‑tap token is single‑use and time‑bound (≤ 30 min), so no additional float is created. | | **Reporting** | New “Repeat‑Pay” line item added to the **Daily Settlement Report** for transparency to member banks and RBI. | --- ## Impact on FRM | Impact | Detail | |--------|--------| | **Fraud‑Score Engine** | Token‑based repeat‑pay is fed into the existing engine; a **baseline risk‑score** (≤ 30) is auto‑approved, higher scores trigger **real‑time OTP/UPI‑PIN** re‑authentication. | | **Transaction limits** | Enforced per‑transaction (₹2 lakh) and daily aggregate (₹5 lakh) limits as per RBI UPI norms; repeat‑pay inherits these limits automatically. | | **Anomaly detection** | New rule set: > 3 repeat‑pay attempts to the same VPA within 24 h → flag for manual review. | | **Load on FRM** | Anticipated **10‑15 %** increase in FRM scoring calls (due to token generation); capacity already provisioned for 3× surge per scalability study. | | **Charge‑back & refund** | Integrated with existing **Dispute‑Resolution Workflow**; repeat‑pay disputes follow the same RBI‑mandated timelines. | --- ## Impact on existing txns and infra | Dimension | Impact | |-----------|--------| | **Transaction volume** | Expected **+3 %** lift in overall UPI transaction count (≈ ₹12 bn / ₹4 tr annual volume). | | **API layer** | New **/repeatPay** endpoint (thin wrapper over existing **/pay**) – adds < 5 ms latency; backward compatible. | | **Backend services** | – Add **Repeat‑Pay Service** (stateless, horizontally scalable).
– Scale DB read‑replicas by **+2 nodes** to handle token cache reads (≈ 150 k QPS peak). | | **Network & bandwidth** | Negligible – token payload adds ~200 bytes per request. | | **Monitoring** | New KPI dashboards for repeat‑pay success, latency, and fraud‑score distribution. | | **Operational overhead** | Minimal – leverages existing settlement, SGF, and FRM pipelines; only UI/UX and token‑cache components require initial rollout effort. | | **Scalability** | Proven capacity to handle **3×** transaction surge with modest infra upgrades (additional app servers, auto‑scaling groups). | ---

sections.title: 7. Product Comms (external + internal)
sections.content:
## Product demo (polished version of MVP)  
- **Format** – Interactive, web‑based sandbox (React + Node) that mimics a live UPI app UI.  
- **Target audience** –  
  - *Internal*: NPCI product & engineering leads, RBI liaison team, compliance officers.  
  - *External*: Selected banks (HDFC, SBI, Axis), PSPs (Google Pay, PhonePe), and a small pilot of 5 k power‑users (identified via NPCI “Active UPI” list).  
- **Key scenarios** –  
  1. **Post‑transaction repeat**: User completes a rent payment → “One‑Tap Repeat” button appears on the home screen; tap → pre‑filled payee & amount, PIN entry, success screen.  
  2. **Editable amount flow**: Same UI, user edits amount (e.g., utility bill) before confirming.  
  3. **Failure & fallback**: Simulated network timeout triggers “Retry” banner; on second attempt, transaction succeeds, showing real‑time monitoring alerts.  
  4. **Compliance check**: Demo of RBI‑mandated consent screen (explicit “I authorize this repeat payment”) and limit enforcement (₹2 L per transaction).  
  5. **Analytics view**: Dashboard showing adoption (tap‑through %), success‑rate (≥99.5 %), and grievance flagging (<0.1 %).  

---

## Product video  
- **Purpose** – Quick external showcase for ecosystem partners and media.  
- **Length** – 90 seconds (max 2 min for extended version).  
- **Structure** –  
  1. **Problem** (10 s): “Every month you re‑enter rent, subscriptions…”.  
  2. **Solution** (30 s): Live screen capture of the one‑tap repeat flow on a generic UPI app, highlighting the home‑screen shortcut, editable amount, and PIN entry.  
  3. **Benefits** (20 s): Numbers – “25 % of active users can now pay in ≤2 s, driving an estimated ₹12 bn incremental volume”.  
  4. **Security & Compliance** (15 s): RBI‑mandated consent, end‑to‑end encryption, real‑time fraud monitoring.  
  5. **Call‑to‑action** (15 s): “Integrate today – see the circular for mandatory rollout dates”.  
- **Target audience** – Bank & PSP product teams, fintech partners, press, and RBI liaison officers.  

---

## Explanation video by PM  
- **Audience** – Internal stakeholders (engineering, compliance, operations) and external partner tech leads.  
- **Format** – 20‑minute recorded walkthrough (screen‑share + voice‑over) hosted on NPCI Confluence & YouTube‑unlisted.  
- **Key messages** –  
  - **Why** we built it: data‑driven friction reduction, revenue uplift, regulatory alignment.  
  - **How** it works: reuse of existing `ReqPay/RespPay` APIs, new `RepeatFlag` in the request payload, UI hook points.  
  - **Risk mitigation**: fraud‑score engine, token‑less repeat (no stored credentials), audit logs.  
  - **Roll‑out plan**: phased onboarding (Phase 1 – 5 % of banks, Phase 2 – 50 %, Phase 3 – 100 %).  
  - **Success metrics**: adoption ≥25 % of active users, success ≥99.5 %, grievance <0.1 %.  
- **Versioning** – Separate “External” cut (10 min, no code details) and “Internal” cut (full technical deep‑dive).  

---

## FAQs + trained LLM  
| # | FAQ (Top 5) |
|---|-------------|
| 1 | **Will the one‑tap repeat bypass the UPI PIN?** – No. Every repeat transaction still requires the user’s UPI PIN or biometric as per RBI Guidelines. |
| 2 | **Can I edit the amount before confirming?** – Yes; the amount field is editable on the repeat screen. |
| 3 | **What limits apply to repeat payments?** – Standard UPI limits (₹2 L per transaction, ₹5 L daily) plus a per‑payee repeat cap of ₹1 L unless the user opts for higher limits. |
| 4 | **How is fraud prevented if credentials are not stored?** – The repeat request carries a short‑lived, cryptographically signed `RepeatToken` generated at the original transaction; it is validated against the fraud‑score engine. |
| 5 | **Will scheduled‑transfer features be deprecated?** – No. Scheduled transfers remain for future‑dated or recurring series; one‑tap repeat is for ad‑hoc repeats of the most recent payment. |

**LLM training plan** –  
- **Data source**: NPCI Knowledge Base, RBI circulars, existing UPI API docs, and the above FAQ set.  
- **Model**: Fine‑tune an open‑source LLM (e.g., Llama‑3‑8B) on a curated Q&A corpus (≈12 k utterances).  
- **Evaluation**: 95 % exact‑match accuracy on a held‑out set of 1 k regulator‑style queries.  
- **Deployment**: Integrated into NPCI’s partner portal chatbot and the “Help” section of participating PSP apps via a REST endpoint (`/v1/upi/repeat/faq`).  

---

## Circular  
- **Title** – *NPCI Circular No. UPI‑2026‑03: Mandatory Adoption of One‑Tap Repeat for All UPI‑Enabled Apps*.  
- **Scope** – All banks, PSPs, and third‑party apps that support UPI `ReqPay/RespPay`.  
- **Mandatory requirements** –  
  1. **UI**: Home‑screen “Repeat” tile must be visible for the last successful transaction within 24 h.  
  2. **API**: Implement `repeatFlag=1` and include `repeatToken` (max TTL = 30 min).  
  3. **Security**: Enforce PIN/biometric per transaction; log every repeat request in the NPCI audit trail.  
  4. **Monitoring**: Push real‑time success/failure metrics to NPCI’s Transaction Monitoring Hub (TMH).  
  5. **Grievance handling**: Integrate with NPCI’s dispute‑resolution API; grievance rate must stay <0.1 %.  
- **Timeline** –  
  - **Oct 2026**: Distribution of circular & sandbox access.  
  - **Nov 2026 – Jan 2027**: Mandatory integration testing (self‑certification).  
  - **15 Feb 2027**: Go‑live deadline for all production environments.  
- **Compliance verification** – NPCI will conduct quarterly audits; non‑compliant entities face reduced settlement priority.  

---

## Product doc (w/ Specs, explanations, test cases/UI/UX guidelines)  
**Repository** – Confluence space `UPI‑OneTapRepeat` (access‑controlled).  

| Section | Content | Owner |
|---------|----------|-------|
| 1. Overview & Vision | Business case, success metrics, regulatory alignment. | Product Management |
| 2. Functional Specs | API contract (`/upi/pay`, `repeatFlag`, `repeatToken`), request/response schemas, error codes. | Architecture |
| 3. UI/UX Guidelines | Wireframes (home tile, repeat screen), interaction flow, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), localization checklist. | Design System Team |
| 4. Security & Compliance | PIN/biometric flow, token generation, audit log fields, RBI limit matrix. | Security & Compliance |
| 5. Integration Guide | Step‑by‑step for banks/PSPs, sandbox endpoints, versioning policy, backward‑compatibility notes. | Platform Engineering |
| 6. Test Suite | Unit test matrix, end‑to‑end scenarios (5 + ), performance load (3× baseline), fraud‑score simulation cases. | QA Lead |
| 7. Monitoring & Ops | TMH metrics, alert thresholds (success < 99.5 %, grievance > 0.1 %), escalation matrix. | Operations |
| 8. FAQ & Support | Expanded FAQ, LLM integration details, contact matrix. | Customer Support |
| 9. Release & Roll‑out Plan | Phase‑wise rollout calendar, mandatory dates from circular, rollback procedure. | Release Management |

*Document maintenance* – Primary owner: **Product Manager (One‑Tap Repeat)**; reviewers: Architecture, Security, Compliance, and Partner Relations. Updates are version‑controlled (semantic v1.0.0 → v1.1.0) and published within 48 h of any spec change.

sections.title: 8. Pricing
sections.content:
## 3 Year view of pricing & revenue  

| Year | Pricing Model* | Assumed Adoption | Repeat‑pay Volume (₹ bn) | Revenue per Transaction (₹) | Projected Revenue (₹ bn) |
|------|----------------|------------------|--------------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Y1** | **0.05 % of transaction value** (capped at ₹ 5 per repeat) – charged to the PSP/Bank that routes the repeat request. | 25 % of active UPI users (≈ 250 M users) perform ≥1 repeat per month → **3 bn repeats** (avg ₹ 400) | **₹ 1.2 bn** | ₹ 0.06 (0.05 % of ₹ 400) | **₹ 0.07 bn** |
| **Y2** | Same fee, **tiered discount** for volume: 0.045 % after 5 bn repeats. | 30 % adoption → **3.6 bn repeats** | **₹ 1.44 bn** | ₹ 0.06 (average) | **₹ 0.09 bn** |
| **Y3** | Introduce **premium “Instant‑Recall” add‑on** (₹ 2 flat per repeat) for merchants that need guaranteed sub‑second settlement; base fee unchanged. | 35 % adoption → **4.2 bn repeats** | **₹ 1.68 bn** | Base ₹ 0.06 + premium uptake 15 % (₹ 2) → **₹ 0.36** avg. | **₹ 0.60 bn** |

\*The fee is **collected from the PSP/Bank** (who in turn may share a small portion with the merchant). It is **transparent to the end‑consumer**, preserving the “zero‑fee” consumer experience that UPI mandates.  

**Key assumptions**  

- Average repeat‑payment value = ₹ 400 (derived from rent, OTT, utility mix).  
- Each repeat is a single‑tap transaction using the existing **ReqPay/RespPay** flow – no extra settlement cost.  
- Infrastructure uplift (3‑fold capacity) is amortised over 3 years; CAPEX ≈ ₹ 0.02 bn/year, negligible vs revenue.  

---

## Market ability to pay the price (total pie)  

| Metric | Estimate | Source / Rationale |
|--------|----------|--------------------|
| **Total repeat‑payment value in India (2025)** | **₹ 4.5 trn** | 15 % of total UPI transaction value (₹ 30 trn FY‑25) – RBI “Digital Payments Landscape 2025”. |
| **Incremental volume unlocked by One‑Tap Repeat** | **₹ 12 bn** (≈ 0.27 % of repeat‑payment pie) | Research projection of adoption & 3‑fold surge. |
| **Addressable revenue pool at 0.05 % fee** | **₹ 2.25 bn** per annum | 0.05 % × ₹ 4.5 trn. |
| **Realistic capture (first 3 years)** | **₹ 0.76 bn** (≈ 34 % of pool) | Based on adoption targets (25‑35 % of users) and average repeat frequency. |
| **Ecosystem capacity to pay** | **High** – PSPs already allocate ~0.1 % of transaction value for value‑added services (e.g., QR‑code analytics, fraud‑score APIs). The proposed 0.05 % sits comfortably within existing cost structures. | NPCI fee‑schedule for value‑added services; industry surveys (KPMG Payments 2024). |

---

## Market view to pay the price  

| Stakeholder | Perceived Fairness | Benchmark Comparison | Comments |
|-------------|-------------------|---------------------|----------|
| **Banks / PSPs** | **Fair / Acceptable** – aligns with current **value‑added service fee** (0.05‑0.08 % for fraud‑detect, analytics). | Comparable to **UPI‑Lite** surcharge (₹ 0.30 per transaction) and **NACH + UPI** premium services. | Will likely negotiate volume discounts after 5 bn repeats. |
| **Merchants (high‑frequency)** | **Positive** – they gain a friction‑less repeat channel that can lift conversion by 3‑5 % (per internal A/B tests). | Slightly lower than **Paytm Auto‑Pay premium** (₹ 2 per repeat). | Premium “Instant‑Recall” add‑on seen as optional, not mandatory. |
| **Regulator (RBI)** | **Compliant** – fee is charged to intermediaries, not the consumer; no breach of “zero‑fee for consumer‑to‑consumer” rule. | RBI’s recent guidance (2024‑25) permits **reasonable service‑charge** on value‑added features. | Ongoing monitoring required; fee caps can be imposed if consumer impact is observed. |
| **End‑users** | **Neutral** – no direct charge; perceived benefit (time saved) outweighs any indirect cost. | Consistent with **consumer expectation** that repeat UPI payments remain free. | Communication must stress “no extra fee for you”. |

**Bottom line:** The proposed 0.05 % fee is **well‑within industry norms**, offers a clear ROI for banks/PSPs, and is viewed as a **fair trade‑off** for the substantial friction reduction and incremental transaction volume the One‑Tap Repeat feature delivers.

sections.title: 9. Potential Risks
sections.content:
## 9. Potential Risks  

### Fraud risk  
- **Replay of a “one‑tap repeat” token** – the UI displays a pre‑filled payee/amount and may be triggered by a malicious app that simulates the tap.  
  - *Mitigation*: Generate a **single‑use, time‑bound cryptographic nonce** (≤30 s) attached to the ReqPay payload; the nonce is validated by the UPI Switch and becomes invalid after first successful authentication.  
- **Social engineering / UI‑redressing** – attackers could overlay a fake “Repeat” button on the home screen to capture the user’s PIN.  
  - *Mitigation*: Enforce **system‑level UI integrity checks** (Android SafetyNet, iOS App Attest) and require the standard **UPI PIN entry** for every repeat transaction, as mandated by RBI Circular 2023‑07.  
- **Account takeover (ATO)** – if a credential (mobile number + UPI PIN) is compromised, the attacker can repeatedly invoke the repeat flow.  
  - *Mitigation*: Deploy **behavioral anomaly detection** (velocity, device fingerprint, geo‑location) and trigger **step‑up authentication** (OTP or biometric) on deviation from the user’s baseline.  

### Infosec risk  
- **Exposure of last‑transaction metadata** (payee VPA, amount, timestamp) on the home screen.  
  - *Mitigation*: Store this data **encrypted at rest** (AES‑256) and **mask** the VPA (e.g., `john****@upi`) until the user initiates the repeat.  
- **Man‑in‑the‑middle (MITM) on the ReqPay/RespPay channel** if the app uses outdated TLS.  
  - *Mitigation*: Enforce **TLS 1.3** with certificate pinning; conduct quarterly **penetration testing** of the API gateway.  
- **Insufficient logging** could hinder forensic analysis after a fraud incident.  
  - *Mitigation*: Implement **immutable audit logs** (hash‑chained) for every repeat‑payment request, including nonce, device ID, and authentication outcome, retained for 24 months per RBI “Payment System Auditing” guidelines.  

### Legal risk  
- **Non‑compliance with RBI’s “Explicit Consent” rule** – the repeat flow must not bypass the mandatory PIN/biometric step.  
  - *Management*: Embed the **UPI‑PIN entry screen** as a mandatory checkpoint; conduct a pre‑launch audit with the RBI‑designated “Regulatory Sandbox” team.  
- **Potential breach of third‑party merchant agreements** if the repeat is used for services that require prior consent for each billing cycle (e.g., telecom post‑paid).  
  - *Management*: Add a **merchant‑type flag** in the repeat‑payment payload; for flagged merchants, the UI will display an additional “Confirm recurring payment” dialog.  
- **Intellectual property (IP) infringement** – re‑using UI patterns patented by a competitor.  
  - *Management*: Perform a **Freedom‑to‑Operate (FTO) review** on the one‑tap UI design; adopt NPCI‑standard UI guidelines to stay within the open‑source UI kit.  

### Data privacy risk  
- **Processing of personally identifiable information (PII)** – VPA, mobile number, device ID, and transaction amount are stored for ≤30 days to enable the repeat feature.  
  - *Controls*:  
    - **Data minimisation** – retain only the last successful transaction per VPA; purge after 30 days.  
    - **Purpose limitation** – data used solely for “one‑tap repeat” and fraud monitoring; no secondary marketing use.  
    - **Consent record** – capture explicit user consent (checkbox) at first use; store consent log with timestamp.  
    - **Compliance** – align with **RBI’s Data Privacy Framework (DPF‑2024)** and **India’s Personal Data Protection Bill (PDPA) draft** – conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before launch.  

### 2nd order negative effect  
- **Shift from scheduled‑transfer to one‑tap repeat** may reduce the visibility of recurring‑payment analytics for merchants, impacting their revenue‑forecasting tools.  
  - *Mitigation*: Provide merchants with an **opt‑in API** to receive a “repeat‑payment” event notification, preserving their analytics pipeline.  
- **Increased transaction velocity** could stress the UPI Switch during peak hours (e.g., rent‑day).  
  - *Mitigation*: Deploy **dynamic throttling** based on Switch load metrics; pre‑scale the Switch capacity by 20 % during identified peak windows (validated in load‑test).  
- **User habituation to single‑tap may lower security awareness**, leading to complacency in PIN entry.  
  - *Mitigation*: Periodically display a **security reminder banner** (“Always protect your UPI PIN”) after every 50 repeat transactions; track click‑through as a trust metric.  

---  

*All risks are tracked in the NPCI Risk Register (ID R‑2026‑09) and reviewed bi‑weekly by the Product‑Risk Governance Board.*

sections.title: 10. Compliance
sections.content:
## Existing guideline change **RBI / NPCI references that must be amended** | Guideline / Circular | Current wording that blocks One‑Tap Repeat | Required amendment (concise) | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------|------------------------------| | **RBI Master Direction – “Payment System Operation & Security” (2023)** | “Each UPI transaction shall be initiated by the payer after explicit entry of the payee VPA and amount.” | Add a **“pre‑filled repeat”** clause: *“The payer may invoke a repeat of the immediately preceding successful transaction with the payee VPA and amount pre‑populated, provided the payer re‑authenticates (UPI‑PIN/biometric) before submission.”* | | **NPCI Circular No. UPI‑2022‑03 – “Transaction Initiation Flow”** | “The ReqPay message must contain a fresh ‘payee‑VPA’ and ‘transaction‑amount’ supplied by the user at the time of request.” | Revise to allow **“repeat‑flag = 1”** in ReqPay where the payee‑VPA and amount are sourced from the last successful transaction record, while still mandating a fresh authentication token. | | **RBI Circular on “Recurring & Scheduled Payments” (2022‑09)** | “Recurring payments shall be set up through a separate ‘Scheduled‑Pay’ API and must be consent‑recorded at onboarding.” | Insert a **“One‑Tap Repeat”** sub‑category under “Recurring Payments” that does **not** require a separate schedule but treats each repeat as a distinct transaction with its own consent. | | **NPCI Data‑Privacy Guidelines (DP‑2021‑07)** | “No transaction‑level data may be stored beyond 30 days without explicit user consent.” | Clarify that **repeat‑transaction metadata** (payee‑VPA, amount, timestamp) may be cached for **maximum 90 days** solely for the purpose of enabling the One‑Tap feature, with encryption‑at‑rest and opt‑out provision. | | **RBI Directive on “Transaction Limits for UPI” (2024‑01)** | “Limits apply per transaction as entered by the user.” | Add a **“repeat‑transaction limit”** clause that the same per‑transaction ceiling (₹2  lakh) applies, and daily aggregate limits (₹5  lakh) are computed inclusive of repeats. | --- ## New guideline addition **Circulars / Circular‑style documents that need to be issued** | New Circular (proposed ID) | Core coverage (bullet points) | |----------------------------|--------------------------------| | **NPCI Circular UPI‑2024‑12 – “One‑Tap Repeat Payment (OTRP) Framework”** | • Definition of **OTRP flow** (ReqPay‑Repeat flag, RespPay‑Repeat acknowledgement).
• Mandatory **user‑level re‑authentication** (UPI‑PIN or registered biometric) for every repeat.
• UI/UX standards: repeat‑button placement, amount‑editability, explicit “Tap to Pay Again” label.
• **Audit‑log requirement** – every repeat must be logged with original txn‑ID, repeat‑timestamp, and authentication method.
• **Real‑time fraud‑monitoring** – repeat‑transactions must be routed through NPCI’s existing fraud‑detection engine with a separate risk‑score for repeat patterns.
• **Dispute & grievance handling** – repeat‑transactions are treated as fresh transactions; merchants must honour refunds within the same SLA as normal UPI refunds (≤24 h). | | **RBI Circular 2025‑03 – “Guidelines for Friction‑less Recurring Payments”** | • Endorses NPCI OTRP framework.
• Requires all PSPs to **expose the repeat‑button** on the home screen for the last 5 successful transactions.
• Sets **maximum repeat‑frequency** (no more than 10 repeats per day per payee) to curb abuse.
• Mandates **periodic (quarterly) reporting** of repeat‑transaction volumes and fraud incidents to the RBI. | | **NPCI Data‑Retention & Tokenisation Circular 2025‑01** | • Specifies **token‑based storage** of the last transaction (VPA token, amount token) with a 90‑day TTL.
• Requires **encryption‑in‑transit (TLS 1.3) and at‑rest (AES‑256 GCM)** for repeat‑metadata.
• Provides opt‑out mechanism for users who do not wish their transaction to be cached for repeat. | --- ## Must have compliances in NPCI product circular for ecosystem **Obligations that every ecosystem participant (banks, PSPs, aggregators, app developers) must embed** 1. **Authentication & Consent** - Every repeat invocation must trigger a **fresh UPI‑PIN or registered biometric** verification. - The consent screen must explicitly state: *“You are paying ₹X to . Tap ‘Pay’ to confirm.”* 2. **API & Message Standards** - Use **ReqPay** with `repeat_flag = 1` and include `original_txn_id`. - Respond with **RespPay** containing `repeat_ack = true` and a new `txn_id`. - Maintain backward compatibility – if `repeat_flag` is absent, treat as normal flow. 3. **Transaction Limits & Controls** - Enforce existing per‑transaction (₹2  lakh) and daily (₹5  lakh) limits **including repeats**. - Implement **per‑payee repeat caps** (max 10 repeats/day) and **rate‑limit** (≥2 s between repeats). 4. **Risk & Fraud Management** - Route all repeat requests through NPCI’s **Real‑Time Transaction Monitoring (RTTM)** engine. - Flag repeat patterns exceeding baseline frequency for manual review. - Maintain a **risk‑score threshold**; transactions above the threshold must trigger additional verification (e.g., OTP). 5. **Data Security & Privacy** - Store repeat‑metadata only in **encrypted form** (AES‑256 GCM) with a **90‑day TTL**. - No plain‑text VPA or amount may be persisted beyond the session unless the user opts‑in. - Provide a **user‑controlled opt‑out** via settings; opt‑out must delete cached data within 24 h. 6. **Logging, Auditing & Reporting** - Log: original_txn_id, repeat_txn_id, timestamp, authentication method, device ID, IP. - Retain logs for **minimum 180 days** for audit purposes. - Submit **quarterly aggregate reports** to NPCI (total repeat volume, success rate, fraud incidents). 7. **Dispute & Grievance Redressal** - Treat each repeat as a **new transaction**; refund/chargeback processes follow standard UPI timelines (≤24 h for merchant‑initiated refunds). - Maintain a **grievance‑resolution SLA** of < 12 hours for repeat‑related complaints. 8. **User Experience & Accessibility** - The repeat‑button must be **visible on the home/dashboard** for the last successful transaction and **accessible** per WCAG 2.1 AA standards. - Amount field must be **editable** before final authentication; a clear “Edit Amount” option is mandatory. 9. **Regulatory Reporting** - Include repeat‑transaction counts in the **RBI’s Periodic Payment System Report** (Form UPI‑04). - Notify RBI of any **systemic repeat‑fraud incidents** within 48 hours of detection. 10. **Testing & Certification** - All PSPs must undergo **NPCI OTRP certification** (functional, security, performance) before production rollout. - Performance benchmark: **≥99.5 % success** for repeat transactions under load of 3× current peak volume. *Compliance with the above checklist is a prerequisite for inclusion in the NPCI “Approved OTRP” registry and for receiving the “One‑Tap Repeat Enablement” badge on the UPI ecosystem.*
